title: Lift-Top Coffee Table with Drawers
summary: A showpiece coffee table in oak with four drawers and a lift-up top. Lots of parts, lots of pocket holes, and clamps — many clamps.
description:
An ambitious coffee table: a hardwood frame carrying four drawers (two per side) under a planked top that lifts on a mechanism so you can work or eat from the couch. The original was built in oak for the show surfaces with cheaper pine/poplar hidden inside — a smart way to get a furniture-grade look without buying oak for parts nobody sees.

This is a build where measuring beats trusting the numbers. Real boards aren't perfectly straight or exactly nominal, so the top glue-up quality depends on the boards you picked, and the interior supports and drawers all get fit to what you actually built. Cut everything first, dry-fit relentlessly, and keep your clamps handy — the frame and top both want clamping while glue and pocket screws set.

Drawers get built to the opening and the 10" slides, set back 3/4" from the front to leave room for an applied face — and the note in the plan is worth heeding: consider trimming about 1/8" off each side of the drawer face so it sits flush inside the frame rather than proud on the outside. The lift-top hardware isn't in the original list because there are several mechanisms; pick one rated for your top's weight and follow its template, because the top is heavy once it's assembled.

tools.note: The frame, drawers, and top are all pocket-holed — a lot of them.
Milling the hardwood to width and ripping clean edges for glue-ups.
So many clamps — the top and frame glue-ups need them.

materials.name: Lift-top mechanism (rated for the top weight)
materials.note: Not in the original list — pick a mechanism and follow its template.

cutList.note: Consider trimming ~1/8" off each side to sit flush inside the frame.

steps.title: Make all the cuts
steps.body: Cut every part to the dimensions in the cut list — those are actual dimensions (0.75 = 3/4", 0.75 x 3.5 x 22.5 is a 1x4 cut to 22-1/2"). Getting all the cutting done and organized up front makes the rest of this parts-heavy build manageable.

steps.title: Glue up the tops
steps.body: Assemble the top panels. If your oak boards are dead straight you'll get a clean fit; if they're not, you'll be fighting gaps, so pick your straightest stock for the top and clamp the glue-up flat. The top has to end up flat because it lifts and gets used as a surface.

steps.title: Build the drawers
steps.body: Build the four drawer boxes: pocket holes join the sides and backs, and a brad nailer attaches the 1/8" plywood bottoms. Don't attach the faces yet — those go on last, fitted to the openings.

steps.title: Drill the pocket holes
steps.body: Get out the jig — this frame has pocket holes everywhere. The 45-1/2" horizontals need them in three spots each (both sides and the middle where the verticals mount), plus the show-side and interior parts. Doing all the pocket-holing in one pass keeps you from missing one and discovering it after assembly.

steps.title: Assemble the side frames
steps.body: Put the sides together. With pocket holes already in the backs of the 1x4s and 1x8s, get your clamps out, start with a leg, and build each side assembly around it — clamped and square before you drive the screws.

steps.title: Mount the front and back rails
steps.body: Mount the long front and back pieces. Set the top 1x4 x 45-1/2" flush to the top of the legs, glued with at least two pocket holes each. These rails tie the two side assemblies into the table frame.

steps.title: Build the interior structure, hang drawers, and set the top
steps.body: Mount the front/back verticals with pocket holes — measure very carefully to find the exact middle of the 45" horizontals so the drawer bays come out even. Add the interior supports and drawer-slide supports, hang the drawers on their 10" slides set 3/4" back from the front, then fit and attach the drawer faces. Finally, install the lift-top mechanism per its template and mount the top. Sand and finish oak-appropriately.
materials: Lift-top mechanism (rated for the top weight)

images.alt: Oak lift-top coffee table with four drawers
